title: Mermaid
review_summary: The reviewer praises "Mermaid" for its inspiring message and impressive cast led by Ellen Burstyn and Jodelle Ferland; it's a true story with notable acting achievements recognized in Emmy nominations, highly recommended to viewers.

title: Left Behind: The Movie
review_summary: The reviewer expresses disappointment in both production value and screenplay quality but acknowledges a few successes; they criticize making an R-rated content into G-rated films for spreading Christian faith. They also reflect on their enjoyment as Southern Baptist minister despite agreeing with negative aspects like bad theology in 'Left Behind' series, implying it's better suited to fiction than serious religious literature.

title: Panic
review_summary: A captivating but perplexing indie drama explores complex relationships amidst moral ambiguity; its clear yet irritating character dynamics leave viewers intrigued despite the lack of direction or closure.

title: Cheeni Kum
review_summary: The reviewer praises Amitabh Bachchan's performance against Tabu as charming but criticizes R Balki’s first film for lacking substantial content beyond witty exchanges in London rain scenes; despite some poignant moments like a girl's cancer subplot, they find it uninspiring after an engaging start.

title: Reign Over Me
review_summary: The reviewer appreciates "Reign Over Me" as a moving drama with humor and depth beyond its surface plot about grief after 9/11. They commend Adam Sandler's acting breakthrough in portraying Charlie Fineman realistically amidst comedic stereotypes, noting Jada Pinkett Smith brings life to her character despite some criticisms of the writing being overshadowed by key moments that reveal deeper themes as they unfold throughout the film
